title: Counterfactual Stop-Now Value Score
summary: Dynamic-programming and game-theoretic F1 papers treat pit timing as a value decision, not just a tyre-age threshold.
rationale: Dynamic-programming and game-theoretic F1 papers treat pit timing as a value decision, not just a tyre-age threshold. A simple counterfactual score for "pit now vs stay out 1-2 more laps" should capture the economics of pit loss, finishability, and yellow-flag optionality more directly than fixed compound windows.
implementation_hint: Estimate track/race-level pit-loss baselines from historical pit laps in training folds, combine them with simple compound degradation templates, and emit features like `best_stop_now_advantage`, `regret_if_wait_1lap`, `best_next_compound_margin`, and `yellow_flag_option_value_proxy`.
expected_effect: Better separation for borderline rows where the car is near, but not yet past, a raw tyre-life threshold.
risk: Pit-loss or degradation templates can leak if computed from the same held-out race/year; cross-fit them strictly by training folds or prior seasons.
